name: Six-Vegetable Curry
recipeIngredient: 1 large Sweet Potato, Peeled and Cut
2 medium Turnips, Peeled and Cut into 3/4-Inch Cubes
12 each String beans, Cut into 2-Inch Pieces
6 Fresh Green Chiles, such as Seeded and De-ribbed If Needed
1 tbsp Molasses, Dissolved in 3 tbsp Water
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the potato, sweet potato, turnips, zucchini, eggplant, and string beans in a large bowl of ice water.
2. Heat the oil in a large enameled cast-iron casserole.
3. Add the onions and cumin seeds; cook over moderately low heat, stirring until the onions soften (7 to 8 minutes).
4. Stir in the chickpea flour and fenugreek; cook, stirring until the chickpea flour becomes a paste and browns slightly (2 to 3 minutes more).
5. Pour in 2 cups of warm water and bring to a boil over moderately high heat.
6. Cook, stirring until the gravy begins to thicken, about 1 minute.
7. Add 2 more cups of water; bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low and cook until the gravy reduces and thickens slightly (about 10 minutes).
8. Stir in the chiles, ginger, ground coriander, turmeric, brown sugar, and salt; cook for 5 minutes longer, stirring gently.
9. Drain the vegetables and add them to the gravy. Stir in the tomatoes and bay leaves; cover and cook until the vegetables are tender but not mushy (about 20 to 30 minutes). If making this dish ahead, undercook the vegetables slightly. Refrigerate up to 2 days. When reheating, add 1/2 cup of water and bring to a simmer over moderate heat before proceeding.
10. Stir in the tamarind mixture and fresh coriander; cook, uncovered for 5 minutes. Discard the bay leaves and serve.
Serve with cooling fresh pineapple for those who do not like the heat.
